page.title: Gusuku Sites and Related Properties of the Kingdom of Ryukyu

revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23989927232|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text: [[ca:Llocs Gusuku i els béns culturals associats del regne de Ryukyu]][[de:Gusuku-Stätten und zugehörige Stätten des Königreichs von Ryukyu]][[en:Gusuku Sites and Related Properties of the Kingdom of Ryukyu]][[es:Sitios Gusuku y bienes culturales asociados del reino de Ryūkyū]][[eu:Gusuku aztarnategiak eta Ryukyuko Erresumako antzeko eremuak]][[fa:سرزمین‌های گوسوکو و دارائی‌های پادشاهی ریوکیو]][[fi:Gusukut ja muut Ryūkyū-kuningaskunnan omistukset]][[fr:Sites gusuku et biens culturels associés du royaume de Ryūkyū]][[gl:Sitios Gusuku e bens culturais asociados do reino de Ryūkyū]][[hy:Ռյուկյու պետության հուշարձանների ցանկ]][[it:Siti Gusuku e beni associati del Regno delle Ryūkyū]][[ja:琉球王国のグスク及び関連遺産群]][[ka:გუსუკუ და რიუკიუს სამეფოს არქიტექტურული ძეგლები]][[ko:류큐 왕국의 구수쿠 유적지과 관련 유산]][[lt:Gusuku vietovės]][[mt:Siti ta' Gusuku u Siti Relatati tar-Renju ta' Ryukyu]][[pt:Sítios Gusuku e Propriedades Relacionadas do Reino de Ryukyu]][[ru:Памятники государства Рюкю]][[sv:Gusukuplatser och områden i kungadömet Ryukyu]][[th:แหล่งกูซูกุและทรัพย์สินที่เกี่ยวข้องของอาณาจักรรีวกีว]][[tr:Ryukyu Krallığı'nın Gusuku Alanları ve İlgili Varlıkları]][[uk:Замки ґуску та дотичні старожитності королівства Рюкю]][[vi:Các di chỉ Gusuku và di sản liên quan của Vương quốc Lưu Cầu]][[xmf:გუსუკუ დო რიუკიუშ ომაფეშ არქიტექტურული ობიექტეფი]][[zh-yue:琉球王國嘅城堡同關連遺產群]][[zh:琉球王國的城堡以及相關遺產群]] {{Infobox UNESCO World Heritage Site | image = Naha Shuri Castle16s5s3200.jpg | image_upright = 1.2 | caption = {{ill|Shuri Castle|en|Shuri Castle|qid=Q907052|lt=Shuri Castle|1=Shuri Castle}} | location = {{ill|Okinawa Prefecture|en|Okinawa Prefecture|qid=Q766445|lt=Okinawa Prefecture|1=Okinawa Prefecture}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} | includes = | criteria = {{UNESCO WHS type|(ii), (iii), (vi)}}(ii), (iii), (vi) | ID = 972 | coordinates = {{coord|26|12|31|N|127|40|58|E|display=inline, title|format=dms}} | year = 2000 | area = {{convert|54.9|ha|acre|abbr=on}} | buffer_zone = {{convert|559.7|ha|acre|abbr=on}} | locmapin = Japan Ryukyu Islands | map_caption = }} The {{nihongo|'''Gusuku Sites and Related Properties of the Kingdom of Ryukyu'''|琉球王国のグスク及び関連遺産群|Ryūkyū ōkoku no gusuku oyobi kanren'isangun}} is an {{ill|UNESCO|en|UNESCO|qid=Q7809|lt=UNESCO|1=UNESCO}} {{ill|World Heritage Site|en|World Heritage Site|qid=Q9259|lt=World Heritage Site|1=World Heritage Site}} which consists of nine sites all located in the {{ill|Okinawa Prefecture|en|Okinawa Prefecture|qid=Q766445|lt=Okinawa Prefecture|1=Okinawa Prefecture}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The heritage sites include two ''[[utaki]]'' (or sacred sites, one a gate and the other a grove), the {{ill|Tamaudun|en|Tamaudun|qid=Q1186324|lt=Tamaudun|1=Tamaudun}} {{ill|Mausoleum|en|Mausoleum|qid=Q162875|lt=Mausoleum|1=Mausoleum}}, one garden, and five ''{{ill|Gusuku|en|Gusuku|qid=Q1044204|lt=Gusuku|1=Gusuku}}'' castles sites, four of which are ruins and one of which is a reconstruction. The sites were inscribed based on the criteria that they were a fine representation of the [[:en:Ryūkyū Kingdom|Ryūkyū Kingdom]]'s culture, whose unique blend of Japanese and Chinese influence made it a crucial economic and cultural junction between several neighboring states.{{cite web | title = Advisory Body Evaluation| version = | publisher = | date = 25 June 1999 | url = https://whc.unesco.org/archive/advisory_body_evaluation/972.pdf | format = pdf | accessdate = 2009-08-01}}{{cite web | title = Gusuku Sites and Related Properties of the Kingdom of Ryukyu — World Heritage List Nomination Cultural Property| publisher = Agency for Cultural Affairs | year = 2000 | url = https://whc.unesco.org/p_dynamic/sites/passfile.cfm?filename=972&filetype=pdf&Category=nominations | accessdate = 2009-08-01}} ==History== The construction of ''gusuku'' began throughout the {{ill|Ryukyu Islands|en|Ryukyu Islands|qid=Q6897416|lt=Ryukyu Islands|1=Ryukyu Islands}} during the Gusuku Period{{cite web | title= Gusuku Period | url= http://www.wonder-okinawa.jp/002/003/e_index.html | work= Wonder Okinawa | accessdate= 2010-01-21 | url-status= dead | archiveurl= https://web.archive.org/web/20100103112921/http://www.wonder-okinawa.jp/002/003/e_index.html | archivedate= 2010-01-03 }} (1187-1314) with the rise of chieftains and feudal lords called ''{{ill|Aji (Ryukyu)|en|Aji (Ryukyu)|qid=Q1028297|lt=Aji|1=Aji (Ryukyu)}}'' at the approach of the 12th century. During this period, people who had been living in coastlines along low-lying areas had moved to higher ground to build villages inland. Agriculture, such as [[pa11y rice]], {{ill|Wheat|en|Wheat|qid=Q15645384|lt=Wheat|1=Wheat}} and {{ill|Millet|en|Millet|qid=Q259438|lt=Millet|1=Millet}}, was developed further during this period. Certain groves within these villages were designated as sacred grounds, called ''[[utaki]]'', for praying to guardian spirits of cultivation. Overseas trading was also opened when the Ryukyu Islands began to create a common culture. The existence of Sueki ware and Chinese ceramics excavated in the {{ill|Amami Islands|en|Amami Islands|qid=Q453510|lt=Amami Islands|1=Amami Islands}} region is considered as strong evidence of its cultural development. At the beginning of the 13th century, a steady rise in the interests of the villages saw the emergence of chieftains who were called ''Aji'' or ''Anji'' and who occupied political positions within these villages. The ''aji'' mainly oversaw taxes and conducted religious rites along with [[Noro (priestess)|Noro priestesses]]. Trade became more developed and persuaded the ''aji'' to gain possession of good harbors like Naha, Yomitan, Itoman, Katsuren, Sashiki, and Unten. The [[:en:Sanzan Period|Sanzan Period]] (1314-1429) began when the regional ''aji'' dealt with struggles to defend their domains as their powers increased. {{ill|Okinawa Island|en|Okinawa Island|qid=Q600614|lt=Okinawa Island|1=Okinawa Island}}{{cite web| title= The Sanzan Period| url= http://www.wonder-okinawa.jp/002/003/e_satto.html| work= Wonder Okinawa| accessdate= 2010-01-21| url-status= dead| archiveurl= https://web.archive.org/web/20081004185539/http://www.wonder-okinawa.jp/002/003/e_satto.html| archivedate= 2008-10-04}} was eventually divided between three competing kingdoms: {{ill|Hokuzan|en|Hokuzan|qid=Q55523|lt=Hokuzan|1=Hokuzan}} in the north with its capital at {{ill|Nakijin Castle|en|Nakijin Castle|qid=Q508866|lt=Nakijin Castle|1=Nakijin Castle}}, {{ill|Chūzan|en|Chūzan|qid=Q55521|lt=Chūzan|1=Chūzan}} in the central area with its capital at {{ill|Urasoe Castle|en|Urasoe Castle|qid=Q2968342|lt=Urasoe Castle|1=Urasoe Castle}}, and {{ill|Nanzan|en|Nanzan|qid=Q55522|lt=Nanzan|1=Nanzan}} in the south with its capital at {{ill|Nanzan Castle|en|Nanzan Castle|qid=Q17052333|lt=Nanzan Castle|1=Nanzan Castle}}. The three kingdoms competed for territory in the {{ill|Okinawa Islands|en|Okinawa Islands|qid=Q697589|lt=Okinawa Islands|1=Okinawa Islands}} and recognition from [[:en:Ming China|Ming China]]. The Sanzan Period ended in 1429 when King {{ill|Shō Hashi|en|Shō Hashi|qid=Q1372130|lt=Shō Hashi|1=Shō Hashi}} conquered the three kingdoms and was recognized by the Chinese Emperor as "King of Ryukyu". The newly formed {{ill|Ryukyu Kingdom|en|Ryukyu Kingdom|qid=Q28025|lt=Ryukyu Kingdom|1=Ryukyu Kingdom}}, with its capital at {{ill|Shuri Castle|en|Shuri Castle|qid=Q907052|lt=Shuri Castle|1=Shuri Castle}}, then conquered neighboring islands, starting with the Amami Islands to the north by the mid 15th century and the {{ill|Sakishima Islands|en|Sakishima Islands|qid=Q697593|lt=Sakishima Islands|1=Sakishima Islands}} by 1500. Gusuku continued to be built during this period, the most notable being {{ill|Nakagusuku Castle|en|Nakagusuku Castle|qid=Q1013209|lt=Nakagusuku Castle|1=Nakagusuku Castle}} and {{ill|Yarazamori Castle|en|Yarazamori Castle|qid=Q25100120|lt=Yarazamori Castle|1=Yarazamori Castle}}. A significant change in status for the kingdom happened in 1609{{cite web | title=Early History of The Ryukyu Kingdom and its Relationship with China and Japan | url=http://shitokai.com/ryukyu.php | work=Shitokai | accessdate=2010-01-21 | url-status=dead | archiveurl=https://archive.today/20120525150704/http://shitokai.com/ryukyu.php | archivedate=2012-05-25 }} with the [[invasion of Ryukyu]] by {{ill|Satsuma Domain|en|Satsuma Domain|qid=Q676187|lt=Satsuma Domain|1=Satsuma Domain}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. Satsuma destroyed both Nakijin Castle and Urasoe Castle, as well as capturing Shuri Castle, during the war. Satsuma won the war, vassalized the Ryukyu Kingdom, and annexed the Amami Islands. Gusuku-building stopped after the 17th century. After {{ill|Empire of Japan|en|Empire of Japan|qid=Q188712|lt=Japan|1=Empire of Japan}} annexed Ryukyu in 1879, some gusuku were used by the {{ill|Imperial Japanese Army|en|Imperial Japanese Army|qid=Q276535|lt=Imperial Japanese Army|1=Imperial Japanese Army}}. During the {{ill|Battle of Okinawa|en|Battle of Okinawa|qid=Q192660|lt=Battle of Okinawa|1=Battle of Okinawa}} in {{ill|Pacific War|en|Pacific War|qid=Q184425|lt=Pacific War|1=Pacific War}}, most of the Gusuku used by the Japanese military, especially Shuri Castle, were damaged or destroyed. Shuri Castle was rebuilt during the 1990s, and in 2000 it and a number of gusuku and other related sites were a11ed to the UNESCO World Heritage Site list. ==Preservation== The world-heritage listing of the "Gusuku Sites and Related Properties of the Kingdom of Ryukyu"{{cite web | title= Japan World Heritage Sites | url=http://www.japan-guide.com/e/e2251.html | work= Japan Guide | accessdate=2010-01-21}}{{cite web| title= Gusuku Sites and related properties of the kingdom of Ryukyu| url= http://www.at.jal.com/world/en/guidetojapan/world_heritage/ryukyu/description/| work= JAL Guide| accessdate= 2010-01-21| archive-url= https://web.archive.org/web/20110713074442/http://www.at.jal.com/world/en/guidetojapan/world_heritage/ryukyu/description/| archive-date= 2011-07-13| url-status= dead}} represents more than 500 years{{cite web | title= World Heritage Committee Inscribes 61 New Sites on World Heritage List | url= http://www.virtualheritage.net/news_blogs/852.htm | work= Virtual Heritage | accessdate= 2010-01-21 | url-status= dead | archiveurl= https://web.archive.org/web/20100707035849/http://www.virtualheritage.net/news_blogs/852.htm | archivedate= 2010-07-07 }}{{cite web | title=Gusuku Sites and Related Properties of the Kingdom of Ryukyu | url= | work= Trip Wolf}} of Ryukyuan history, specifically from the 12th to the 17th century. On November 30, 2000, UNESCO inscribed it, along with 60 other global sites, on the World Heritage list. It was selected on the basis of three of the 10 criteria{{cite web | title= Criteria for Selection | url= https://whc.unesco.org/en/criteria| work= Unesco | accessdate=2010-01-21}}{{cite web | title=World Heritage Comm. Enshrines 61 new Sites | url=https://whc.unesco.org/en/news/184 | work= Unesco| accessdate=2010-01-21}} set by UNESCO. Specifically, the Gusuku sites were selected for the way that they (1) exhibit the importance of human values over a span of time (Criteria ii), in this case covering more than 500 years of cultural and ethnic history; (2) show an exceptional cultural tradition by a civilization that has disappeared (Criteria iii); and (3) are regarded for tangibly linking events and traditions with ideals and beliefs with literary and artistic works (Criteria vi). == List of sites == {| class="wikitable sortable" width="100%" style="background:#ffffff;" |- !width="38%" align="left" style="background:#ffdead;" | Name !width="17%" align="left" style="background:#ffdead;" | Type !width="25%" align="left" style="background:#ffdead;" | Location !width="20%" align="left" style="background:#ffdead;" class="unsortable"|Picture |- | {{Nihongo|{{ill|Shuri Castle|en|Shuri Castle|qid=Q907052|lt=Shuri Castle|1=Shuri Castle}} Site|首里城跡|Shuri-jō ato|Okinawan: ''Sui Gushiku''}} | gusuku ruins''Reconstructed {{ill|Gusuku|en|Gusuku|qid=Q1044204|lt=Gusuku|1=Gusuku}} on site of ruins'' | Naha, Okinawa | [[Image:Naha_Shuri_Castle51s3s4200.jpg|150px]] |- | {{Nihongo|{{ill|Nakijin Castle|en|Nakijin Castle|qid=Q508866|lt=Nakijin Castle|1=Nakijin Castle}} Site|今帰仁城跡|Nakijin-jō ato|Okinawan: ''Nachizin Gushiku''}} | gusuku ruins''{{ill|Gusuku|en|Gusuku|qid=Q1044204|lt=Gusuku|1=Gusuku}}'' ruins | kunigami nakijin{{ill|Nakijin, Okinawa|en|Nakijin, Okinawa|qid=Q1184661|lt=Nakijin|1=Nakijin, Okinawa}}, {{ill|Kunigami District, Okinawa|en|Kunigami District, Okinawa|qid=Q1330132|lt=Kunigami|1=Kunigami District, Okinawa}}, Okinawa | [[Image:Nakijinjyousi2.jpg|150px]] |- | {{Nihongo|{{ill|Zakimi Castle|en|Zakimi Castle|qid=Q2338956|lt=Zakimi Castle|1=Zakimi Castle}} Site|座喜味城跡|Zakimi-jō ato|Okinawan: ''Zachimi Gushiku''}} | gusuku ruins''{{ill|Gusuku|en|Gusuku|qid=Q1044204|lt=Gusuku|1=Gusuku}}'' ruins | nakagami yomitan[[:en:Yomitan, Okinawa|Yomitan]], {{ill|Nakagami District, Okinawa|en|Nakagami District, Okinawa|qid=Q1205862|lt=Nakagami|1=Nakagami District, Okinawa}}, Okinawa | [[Image:Zakimigusuku.jpg|150px]] |- | {{Nihongo|{{ill|Katsuren Castle|en|Katsuren Castle|qid=Q2471589|lt=Katsuren Castle|1=Katsuren Castle}} Site|勝連城跡跡|Katsuren-jō ato|Okinawan: ''Katchin Gushiku''}} | gusuku ruins''{{ill|Gusuku|en|Gusuku|qid=Q1044204|lt=Gusuku|1=Gusuku}}'' ruins | nakagami uruma[[:en:Uruma, Okinawa|Uruma]], {{ill|Nakagami District, Okinawa|en|Nakagami District, Okinawa|qid=Q1205862|lt=Nakagami|1=Nakagami District, Okinawa}}, Okinawa | [[Image:KatsurenGusukuRuins.jpg|150px]] |- | {{Nihongo|{{ill|Nakagusuku Castle|en|Nakagusuku Castle|qid=Q1013209|lt=Nakagusuku Castle|1=Nakagusuku Castle}} Site|中城城跡|Nakagusuku-jō ato|Okinawan: ''Nakagusiku Gushiku''}}{{Cite web|url=http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/17007|title=ふたつの中城御殿跡|last=Okinawa Prefectural reserve cultural assets center|date=2015|website=Comprehensive Database of Archaeological Site Reports in Japan|publisher=|access-date=2016-09-02}} | gusuku ruins''{{ill|Gusuku|en|Gusuku|qid=Q1044204|lt=Gusuku|1=Gusuku}}'' ruins | nakagami nakagusuku{{ill|Nakagusuku, Okinawa|en|Nakagusuku, Okinawa|qid=Q1205406|lt=Nakagusuku|1=Nakagusuku, Okinawa}}, {{ill|Nakagami District, Okinawa|en|Nakagami District, Okinawa|qid=Q1205862|lt=Nakagami|1=Nakagami District, Okinawa}}, Okinawa | [[Image:Nakagusukuview.jpg|150px]] |- | {{nihongo|{{ill|Tamaudun|en|Tamaudun|qid=Q1186324|lt=Tamaudun|1=Tamaudun}}|玉陵}} | mausoleum{{ill|Mausoleum|en|Mausoleum|qid=Q162875|lt=Mausoleum|1=Mausoleum}} | naha[[:en:Naha, Okinawa|Naha]], {{ill|Okinawa Prefecture|en|Okinawa Prefecture|qid=Q766445|lt=Okinawa|1=Okinawa Prefecture}} |[[File:Tamaudun02s3s4592.jpg|150px]] |- | {{nihongo|[[Sonohyan-utaki]] Ishimon|園比屋武御嶽石門||{{ill|Okinawan language|en|Okinawan language|qid=Q34233|lt=Okinawan|1=Okinawan language}}: ''Sunuhwan-utaki''}} | utakiStone gate at ''[[utaki]]'' | nahaNaha, Okinawa |[[Image:Naha Shuri Castle04bs3200.jpg|150px]] |- | {{nihongo|[[:en:Shikinaen|Shikinaen]]|識名園}} | gardenGarden | nahaNaha, Okinawa | [[Image:Palace and Rokkaku-do, Shikinaen 2002-03-20.jpg|150px]] |- | {{nihongo|[[Sefa-utaki]]|斎場御嶽|Seefa-utaki}} | utaki''[[Utaki]]'' | nanjo[[:en:Nanjō, Okinawa|Nanjō]], Okinawa | [[Image:Ujyoguchi2.jpg|150px]] |} ==See also== *{{ill|Gusuku|en|Gusuku|qid=Q1044204|lt=Gusuku|1=Gusuku}} *[[:en:Ryūkyū Kingdom|Ryūkyū Kingdom]] *{{ill|Tourism in Japan|en|Tourism in Japan|qid=Q1060220|lt=Tourism in Japan|1=Tourism in Japan}} * publisher=Kodansha| location=Tokyo| isbn=0-87011-766-1}} *Kerr, George H. (2000). Okinawa: the History of an Island People. (revised ed.) Boston: Tuttle Publishing. *Smits, Gregory (1999). "Visions of Ryukyu: Identity and Ideology in Early-Modern Thought and Politics." Honolulu: University of Hawai'i Press. == References == {{reflist}} == External links == {{commonscat-inline|Gusuku sites and related properties of the Kingdom of Ryukyu}} *[https://whc.unesco.org/en/list/972/ ''Gusuku Sites and Related Properties of the Kingdom of Ryukyu'' at UNESCO] culturals associats del regne de Ryukyu]] {{Commons category|琉球王国のグスク及び関連遺産群}} [[es:Sitios Gusuku y bienes culturales asociados del reino de Ryūkyū]] [[eu:Gusuku aztarnategiak eta Ryukyuko Erresumako Relacionadas do Reino de Ryukyu]] [[ru:Памятники государства Рюкю]] [[sv:Gusukuplatser och områden i kungadömet Ryukyu]] [[th:แหล่งกูซูกุและทรัพย์สินที่เกี่ยวข้องของอาณาจักรรีวกีว]] [[tr:Ryukyu Krallığı'nın Gusuku Alanları ve İlgili Varlıkları]] [[uk:Замки ґуску та дотичні старожитності королівства Рюкю]] [[vi:Các di chỉ Gusuku và di sản liên quan của Vương quốc Lưu Cầu]] [[xmf:გუსუკუ დო რიუკიუშ ომაფეშ არქიტექტურული ობიექტეფი]] [[zh:琉球王國的城堡以及相關遺產群]] [[zh-yue:琉球王國嘅城堡同關連遺產群]] {{wikidata link|Q1333330}} [[Category:Castles in Okinawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Ryukyuan culture]]
